input.description: Fetch Done items from project 718 via GraphQL

content.text:
Fetching full issue bodies to analyze HOW vs WHAT framing.

[REDACTED]

input.command:
cd /workspaces/agno && set -a && source .env 2>/dev/null; set +a && uv run python - <<'PY'
import asyncio, os, json
import httpx

ISSUES = [
    ("coaching-dashboard", 32),
    ("coaching-dashboard", 13),
    ("coaching-dashboard", 26),
    ("coaching-dashboard", 29),
    ("coaching-dashboard", 11),
    ("coaching-dashboard", 10),
    ("coaching-dashboard", 12),
    ("coaching-dashboard", 4),
    ("coaching-dashboard", 7),
    ("coaching-dashboard", 23),
    ("coaching-dashboard", 27),
]

token = os.environ.get("GITHUB_TOKEN", "")
headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}", "Accept": "application/vnd.github+json"}

async def main():
    async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=60) as http:
        for repo, num in ISSUES:
            r = await http.get(f"https://api.github.com/repos/trilogy-group/{repo}/issues/{num}", headers=headers)
            d = r.json()
            print("="*80)
            print(f"# {num}: {d.get('title')}")
            print(f"URL: {d.get('html_url')}")
            print("-"*80)
            print(d.get('body') or "(no body)")
            print()

asyncio.run(main())
PY
input.description: Fetch full bodies of all Done issues

input.command: cd /workspaces/agno && set -a && source .env 2>/dev/null; set +a && for n in 26 29 11; do echo "===== ISSUE $n ====="; gh api repos/trilogy-group/coaching-dashboard/issues/$n --jq '.body' 2>/dev/null; echo; done

content.text:
I pulled all **11 Done** items from [coaching-dashboard — Kanban](https://github.com/orgs/trilogy-group/projects/718) and reviewed each body for **HOW** (prescribed implementation) vs **WHAT** (outcomes/constraints).

## Clear micromanagement (HOW > WHAT)

These read like implementation tickets, not outcome tickets.

| Issue | Title | Why it's micromanaged |
|-------|-------|----------------------|
| [#29](https://github.com/trilogy-group/coaching-dashboard/issues/29) | Canonical API errors for REST + MCP parity | Prescribes exact files (`platform/api-error.ts`, `mcp/tool-result.ts`), function names (`throwApiError`, `okToolResult`, …), TypeScript interfaces, invoke-block patterns, layering table, and a migration checklist. The symptom is clear; the fix is fully architected for you. |
| [#26](https://github.com/trilogy-group/coaching-dashboard/issues/26) | Coach domain — CRUD + own-profile (REST + MCP) | Specifies schema fields, enum values (`Coach`/`HoA`/…), defaults (`daily_cap: 8`), timezone (`America/Chicago`), which fields are self-editable vs admin-only, soft-delete via `active = false`, scope names (`coach:read`/`coach:write`), and a `/me` route. That's a data model + API spec, not just "admins manage coaches; coaches edit their own profile." |
| [#7](https://github.com/trilogy-group/coaching-dashboard/issues/7) | CI flake: Supabase 502 after db reset | Drops in a complete `wait_for_supabase_healthy()` bash function: URL, retry count, sleep interval, and health semantics (1xx–4xx = healthy). The WHAT ("don't proceed until Supabase is up") is one line; the HOW is the whole issue. |
| [#4](https://github.com/trilogy-group/coaching-dashboard/issues/4) | Tighten coverage include | Copy-paste `vite.config.ts` block with exact `include`/`exclude` paths and threshold numbers. Reasoning is good; the solution is pre-written. |
| [#23](https://github.com/trilogy-group/coaching-dashboard/issues/23) | Add deploy-cloudrun.yml to path filters | Literally "add one line" with the exact YAML snippet. Problem is well stated; fix is fully specified. |
| [#12](https://github.com/trilogy-group/coaching-dashboard/issues/12) | Cloud Run deploy wipes runtime env vars | Step-by-step: which `env:` keys, `--set-env-vars` wiring, fail-fast guard, and exact `gh variable set` commands. Infra chores often get this treatment, but it's still HOW-heavy. |

## Borderline (stack/approach chosen, outcomes still matter)

| Issue | Title | Verdict |
|-------|-------|---------|
| [#10](https://github.com/trilogy-group/coaching-dashboard/issues/10) | Convert Angular/AnalogJS to Next.js | Prescribes Next.js App Router and what to keep (Supabase, MCP). Mitigating factor: actual deliverable is feasibility + design doc, not "go rewrite the app." |
| [#27](https://github.com/trilogy-group/coaching-dashboard/issues/27) | Google sign-in via Supabase Auth | Names Supabase Auth + Google OAuth and JWT validation as the mechanism. Definition of Done is mostly behavioral (sign in, 401 unauthenticated, verified email available). Reasonable for an auth-foundation slice, but not implementation-agnostic. |

## Mostly WHAT-focused (not micromanaged)

| Issue | Title | Why |
|-------|-------|-----|
| [#32](https://github.com/trilogy-group/coaching-dashboard/issues/32) | System overview → produce the domain map | Context + deliverable (`design/domain-map.md` with domains, entities, seams). Architecture principles (MCP-first, RBAC at data layer) are product constraints, not step-by-step build instructions. |
| [#13](https://github.com/trilogy-group/coaching-dashboard/issues/13) | Chore: delete unnecessary scripts | Lists files to remove — that's the entire scope, not how to implement a feature. |
| [#11](https://github.com/trilogy-group/coaching-dashboard/issues/11) | Chore: delete unnecessary scripts if they exist | Same as #13. |

---

## Pattern

The board mixes two issue styles:

1. **Product/domain slices** (#26, #27, #32) — some outcome-oriented, some over-specified at the field/API level.
2. **Infra/tooling chores** (#4, #7, #12, #23, #29) — almost always copy-paste solutions.

**Worst offenders:** #29 and #26. #29 is a mini design doc with file paths and code; #26 is a domain spec disguised as an issue.

**Best examples of WHAT-not-HOW:** #32 (domain map) and the chore tickets (#11, #13).

If you want a simple rule for future issues: state the **observable behavior** and **acceptance criteria**; leave file names, function names, and code blocks to the implementer unless the constraint is non-negotiable (e.g. "must use Supabase Auth" for #27).

[REDACTED]
<user_query>Briefly inform the user about the task result and perform any follow-up actions (if needed). If there's no follow-ups needed, don't explicitly say that.</user_query>

The `gh auth refresh` for `read:project` didn’t finish — it stopped at the device login step (`60F3-48AD` at https://github.com/login/device), which needs interactive approval.

That didn’t block the board review: project 718 was read with the `GITHUB_TOKEN` from `.env`, which already had the right access. The Done-issue HOW vs WHAT analysis stands as delivered.

content.text:
There are **12 open items** on [coaching-dashboard — Kanban](https://github.com/orgs/trilogy-group/projects/718): **2 Ready**, **10 Todo**. Same HOW vs WHAT lens as before.

## Not applicable (operational, not build work)

| Issue | Status | Notes |
|-------|--------|-------|
| [#21](https://github.com/trilogy-group/coaching-dashboard/issues/21) PM Journal | Todo | PM run log sink — not a feature spec |
| [#22](https://github.com/trilogy-group/coaching-dashboard/issues/22) PM cannot pick a goal | Todo | PM blocker notice — not a feature spec |

---

## Clear micromanagement (HOW > WHAT)

### Ready

| Issue | Why |
|-------|-----|
| [#34](https://github.com/trilogy-group/coaching-dashboard/issues/34) Principal & role-assignment registry | Full schema (`email`, `roles[]`, `active`), exact scope map (`admin → *:write`, `coach → coach:read:self`, …), bootstrap via env seed emails, MCP tool names (`whoami`, `assign_role`, …), and specific HTTP codes. Reads like an API spec, not "admins manage who has which role." |
| [#35](https://github.com/trilogy-group/coaching-dashboard/issues/35) Audit-log infrastructure | Prescribes implementation shape: "repo decorator / service middleware is the **only** way mutations run," exact audit fields, write-before-success with rollback, append-only enforcement. The outcome ("every mutation is auditable") is clear; the architecture is decided for you. |

### Todo — domain slices (same pattern as Done #26)

All of these are field-level specs with scopes, MCP tool names, and RBAC rules spelled out:

| Issue | Micromanagement highlights |
|-------|---------------------------|
| [#36](https://github.com/trilogy-group/coaching-dashboard/issues/36) Admin-config | Exact keys/defaults (`weekly_target.CampusDRI = 20`, `downtrend_window_weeks = 3`), typed value model, MCP tool names |
| [#38](https://github.com/trilogy-group/coaching-dashboard/issues/38) Guide domain | Full schema, assignment link model, `source: override \| api`, MCP tool inventory |
| [#39](https://github.com/trilogy-group/coaching-dashboard/issues/39) Support messages | Thread model, status enum, notification wiring to Google Chat, scope names |
| [#40](https://github.com/trilogy-group/coaching-dashboard/issues/40) Session domain | Full field list, **derived-status algorithm spelled out** (Scheduled → InProgress → AwaitingNotes → …), timezone, proctoring flag behavior |
| [#42](https://github.com/trilogy-group/coaching-dashboard/issues/42) Post-call DS form | Per-contributor field lists, three call-type shapes with enum values, visibility rules, draft autosave |

### Todo — integration slices (worst offenders)

| Issue | Why it's especially HOW-heavy |
|-------|------------------------------|
| [#41](https://github.com/trilogy-group/coaching-dashboard/issues/41) OpenCal integration | Points to specific files in `trilogy-group/opencal` (`webhookDelivery.ts`, `route.ts`, `schema.ts`), exact webhook body shape, `X-OpenCal-Signature: sha256=…` HMAC scheme, retry backoff (1s/2s/4s), exact event enum. Integration contract doc, not an outcome ticket. |
| [#43](https://github.com/trilogy-group/coaching-dashboard/issues/43) QC integration | Exact AVC endpoint, file paths in `alpha-video-analysis`, **step-by-step PASS/FAIL derivation** from `callRating.ts` (three boolean criteria, fallbacks, regex). The "admin reads QC verdict, gracefully pending when unavailable" outcome is buried under implementation. |

---

## Borderline

| Issue | Status | Verdict |
|-------|--------|---------|
| [#37](https://github.com/trilogy-group/coaching-dashboard/issues/37) Observability | Todo | Names Sentry + Google Chat + throttling, but Definition of Done is behavioral (errors captured, scrubbed, alerted). Tech choices are constraints, not copy-paste code. Least micromanaged of the build issues. |

---

## Summary

**10 of 12** open issues are build specs, and **9 of those 10** micromanage implementation heavily — same style as Done #26.

**Worst on the board (open or done):** integration issues **#41** and **#43**, plus the pending **#35** (prescribes decorator/middleware pattern).

**Only relatively clean build issue:** **#37** (observability outcomes, tooling named but not architected).

**Ready queue is entirely micromanaged:** both #34 and #35 are detailed specs, not outcome-only tickets.

If you're trying to give agents or engineers room to design: the domain-slice template (#34–#42) and integration contract sections (#41, #43) are the main things to trim — keep Definition of Done and behavioral rules, drop schema fields, MCP tool names, and external file references unless they're true cross-repo contracts.
